summary: AI respawn has separate vanilla and non-vanilla implementations; feature work must source-check and smoke both branches or explicitly scope one out.
nextAction: Add vanilla/non-vanilla AI respawn smoke cases to the testing workflow.

summary: Commander order UI writes public group variables through common setters; do not assume a server-authoritative command queue until the live executor path is proven.
nextAction: Source-check and smoke commander AI order execution before hardening or extending order variables.

summary: Some server cleanup loops drain replicated variables written by clients; queue fixes must be idempotent under repeated publication and hosted/dedicated locality.
nextAction: Add empty-vehicle repeated-publication and hosted/dedicated locality checks to cleanup validation.

summary: Cleanup patches must cite producer and consumer array shapes; current mine cleanup removes nested pairs with a flat subtraction shape and garbage flags use different variable names.
nextAction: Patch or document garbage flag alignment and mine pair removal with source-only plus runtime cleanup smoke.

summary: Config edits propagate through root faction files, gear registries, loadout templates, side/factory unit arrays, AI squads/loadouts, upgrade levels, derived prices and generated missions.
nextAction: Promote into a config data-model checklist or dedicated atlas section.

summary: Module patches must identify whether the runtime edge is boot init, respawn reapply, unit creation attach, PV/PVF event or server loop; smoke that edge rather than only the edited module file.
nextAction: Add runtime-edge labels to module owner pages as modules are next touched.
